channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Hillary Clinton Says Mass Migration Went ‘Too Far’ But Here’s How You Can Tell She Doesn’t Mean It
item.description: Only when elections are at stake do Democrats pretend to notice the consequences of mass migration.

item.title: Congress Should Close the Big Hospitals Tax Loophole
item.description: Large nonprofit hospital networks are leveraging huge subsidies for financial gains and political advantage.

item.title: Anthropic was supposed to be a ‘safe’ alternative to OpenAI, but CEO Dario Amodei admits his company struggles to balance safety with profits
item.description: Anthropic’s CEO says intense commercial pressure is testing the company’s safety-first AI mission.

item.title: From deportation to court, key events in Kilmar Abrego Garcia's fight with the Trump administration
item.description: Kilmar Abrego Garcia entered the news in March 2025 after he was deported to El Salvador despite a court ruling that should have prevented it. His com...

item.title: Trump won’t let Dem sewage 'failures' slide as Moore faces heat over infrastructure grade
item.description: Raw sewage spill in Potomac River reaches bacteria levels 2,700 times safe limit after pipeline collapse, sparking political battle between Trump and ...

item.title: Judge orders migrant deported in 'error' free from ICE custody with criminal case looming
item.description: The judge ruled that Trump administration officials have done "nothing" to prove Abrego Garcia's continued ICE detention would be in line with due pro...

item.title: Trump administration is erasing history and science at national parks, lawsuit argues
item.description: Conservation and historical organizations sued the Trump administration on Tuesday over National Park Service policies that the groups say erase histo...

item.title: Ray Dalio warns of ‘great disorder’ period for world economy, marked by ‘clash of great powers’—just like the 1930s
item.description: The billionaire hedge fund manager warned that the world has entered a historical cycle that he believes is the most turbulent and violent possible.

item.title: Thousands of CEOs just admitted AI had no impact on employment or productivity—and it has economists resurrecting a paradox from 40 years ago
item.description: In the 1980s, economist Robert Solow made an observation that reminded economists of today’s AI boom: “You can see the computer age everywhere but in ...

item.title: Hunt files police report against Cornyn campaign staffer over alleged family 'doxxing' incident
item.description: Texas Senate candidate Wesley Hunt filed a police report after an opponent's campaign staffer allegedly posted his personal info on social media.

item.title: Attacking America Abroad, Hillary Clinton Is The Eileen Gu Of Politics
item.description: Hillary was the vessel into which Europeans could pour their refusal to accept American political change.

item.title: Companies are cycling through CEOs—and replacing them with first-timers
item.description: The year of the rookie CEO has arrived.

item.title: $56 trillion national debt leading to a spiraling crisis: Budget watchdog warns the U.S. is walking a crumbling path
item.description: Trump is putting the U.S. economy on a "sugar high" and leaving other people holding the bill when it comes due a decade from now, according to the CR...

item.title: AOC’s Risible Performance
item.description: AOC’s time at the Munich conference was another reminder that no matter how much she is billed as a rising star, she is still callow and unserious.

item.title: 'I support all three': Trump declines to endorse one Texas GOP Senate candidate
item.description: President Trump has not decided which GOP candidate to endorse ahead of the contentious Republican Senate primary in Texas, instead opting to favor al...

item.title: Obama dragged for 'headache'-inducing presidential center update that has visitors squinting
item.description: Barack Obama's Chicago presidential center generates controversy over architectural design and gentrification concerns. The campus includes a museum t...

item.title: America Seeks Partner, Europe Swipes Left
item.description: There is a kind of romantic streak to U.S. relations with Europe in the Trump era.

item.title: Two More Trans Killers Carry Out Atrocities
item.description: There are dots here that should be connected.

item.title: Schumer, Dems' goal is to 'tie people down' so they can't campaign, Thune says
item.description: Every day campaigning matters ahead of the 2026 midterm elections, and Senate Majority Leader John Thune, R-S.D., contended the Democrats are harming ...

item.title: Conservative Law Firm Picks Up Dobbs Litigator To Fuel More Supreme Court Victories
item.description: A key member of the legal team that convinced the Supreme Court to overrule Roe v. Wade has joined a new law firm to pursue a broader range of appella...

item.title: Exclusive: New York Times Hit Piece On ICE Facilities’ Medical Care Is Riddled With Falsehoods, DHS Says
item.description: 'These allegations of illegal aliens being denied proper medical care in ICE custody are FALSE,' Dr. Sean Conley, DHS chief medical officer, told The ...

item.title: Judge blocks ICE from rearresting Kilmar Abrego Garcia for 'foreseeable future'
item.description: A federal judge ruled Tuesday that the Trump administration has been playing games with Kilmar Abrego Garcia, and issued a new order barring ICE from ...

item.title: Corporate Media Give Trans Hockey Game Shooter The ‘Gunperson’ Treatment
item.description: Details of Dorgan's trans identity have been deemed unpublishable by corporate media outlets which refuse to call him a man.

item.title: Rubio’s Impressive Tightrope Walk
item.description: The secretary of state stayed true to President Trump while reassuring Europe.

item.title: Minnesota's Legislature braces for a federal immigration fight as the enforcement surge winds down
item.description: Democrats hope to rein in the power of federal immigration officers in Minnesota as the state's legislative session gets underway Tuesday, while Repub...

item.title: Social Security Claiming Strategies for High-Net-Worth Clients
item.description: Social Security claiming is a capital-allocation decision for affluent clients. This analysis weighs taxes, longevity risk, and liquidity trade-offs. ...

item.title: Evolving Your Wealth Management Practice for 2026 and Beyond
item.description: Wealth management’s 2026 outlook: growth will hinge on transparency, integration, and relevance to women and next-generation investors. The post Evolv...

item.title: AI Is Reshaping Bank Risk
item.description: AI is exposing the limits of legacy bank controls, making governance quality critical to resilience and investor confidence. The post AI Is Reshaping ...

item.title: What Makes an Ideal Leveraged Buyout Candidate?
item.description: In competitive PE markets, successful LBOs depend on durable business models, recurring cash flows, defensible market positions, and disciplined targe...

item.title: Rethinking Household Asset Allocation Under Capital Constraints
item.description: Leveraged ETFs aren’t about more risk, they’re about using less capital to achieve a desired risk profile in household portfolios. The post Rethinking...
